commit.message:
Security audit fixes: CORS, CSP, MDX injection, API hardening

- Restrict CORS to shelby-ai.com origins (was wildcard *)
- Block JS execution in MDX content (was explicitly allowed)
- Remove unsafe-eval from CSP header
- Strip debug info from API error responses
- Add email format validation and length limit
- Quote GitHub Actions inputs to prevent shell injection
- Replace git add -A with explicit paths in CI
- Fix broken pricing table buttons (href="#" → disabled style)
- Delete redundant api.env file

commit.message:
fix(ego-lint): reduce false positives for R-SLOP-35, R-SEC-03, R-SLOP-13 (#123)

## Summary

Reduces false positives for three zero-precision pattern rules
identified during field test annotation (#109):

- **R-SLOP-35** (`Object.freeze`): Added guard for
`Object.freeze(varName)` — freezing an existing variable is defensive
programming, not AI slop. Still fires on inline `Object.freeze({...})`.
Eliminates 1 FP (dash-to-panel).
- **R-SEC-03** (HTTP URLs): Added `skip-comments: true` so HTTP URLs in
`//` and `/* */` comments (license headers, attribution) are skipped.
Still fires on HTTP URLs in actual code. Eliminates 2 FPs
(dash-to-panel).
- **R-SLOP-13** (`this instanceof`): Added guard for `const x = this
instanceof Y` — variable assignment indicates polymorphic dispatch
(method shared across prototypes), not a redundant check. Eliminates 2
FPs (v-shell).

## Test plan

- [x] 715 tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] New fixture `instanceof-dispatch@test` tests both guard
(variable-assigned instanceof suppressed) and still-firing case
(always-true instanceof in class method)
- [x] Updated `slop-object-freeze@test` with `Object.freeze(monitors)`
guard case
- [x] Updated `gpl-license-header@test` with block comment HTTP URL
- [ ] Field test verification on affected extensions

Related: #120 (R-SLOP-38 tracked separately)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: port Python tests to Rust (batch 3+4: universal, varfont, googlefonts) (#702)

Port 26 Python test functions to Rust across 27 check files.
74 new Rust test functions added, covering checks for:
canonical_filename, color_fonts, description (eof_linebreak, git_url,
has_unsupported_elements, min_length, urls, valid_html), fstype, gasp,
has_ttfautohint_params, meta/script_lang_tags, name (description_max_length,
family_name_compliance, familyname_first_char, line_breaks, mandatory_entries,
rfn, version_format), render_own_name, unitsperem, use_typo_metrics,
varfont/has_HVAR, vendor_id, weightclass, axisregistry/fvar_axis_defaults.

All 126 tests pass. Deleted 26 corresponding Python test functions.

commit.message:
Fix entry duplication by preserving client-generated UUIDs in all POST routes

When client sends an id in the POST body, use it as the record id instead of
letting the server generate a new one. Prevents sync engine from creating
duplicate records in IndexedDB.

commit.message:
Add email OTP login and admin dashboard for realtime permission management

- Add users collection to MongoDB with email, user_uid, realtime_enabled fields
- Implement 4-digit OTP login flow: POST /auth/send-otp and POST /auth/verify-otp
- OTP stored in Redis with 10-minute TTL; sent via aiosmtplib SMTP or logged in dev mode
- Add GET /login page (two-step email then code form)
- Add GET /dashboard for admin users (configured via ADMIN_EMAILS in EMAIL_SETTINGS)
- Add POST /api/users/{email}/realtime to toggle realtime transcription permission
- Update is_realtime_authorized to check users_collection.realtime_enabled via user_uid
- Add EMAIL_SETTINGS to config.example.py with ADMIN_EMAILS and SMTP settings
- Add aiosmtplib>=3.0.0 dependency
- Existing realtime_tokens auth remains unchanged for backward compatibility

commit.message:
Fix asset_file_path signature and upload timing in skills

- run-ml-execution: Fix wrong asset_file_path call to use (asset_name, file_name)
  instead of (filename, description=...)
- catalog-operations-workflow: Move upload_execution_outputs() outside with block
  in all 5 script templates to match actual API requirement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Proper date parsing for all Google Sheets date formats

- Normalize dates to ISO 8601 at sync time in google-sheets.ts so Redis
  always contains clean, reliably parseable date strings. Handles:
  · Standard formats (ISO, M/D/YYYY, M/D/YYYY H:MM:SS)
  · 12-hour AM/PM format ("3/9/2026 2:30:00 PM") — Node.js can't parse this
  · DD/MM/YYYY format when day > 12 (e.g. "27/02/2026") — Node.js fails too

- Stale client alert now shows the date of the last meeting-ready lead
  next to each client name, making it clear whether the alert is real or
  still a false positive.

- After a Refresh sync the new normalized dates take effect immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve 409 sync conflict and text node render crash

- users/sync: handle P2002 by claiming the existing email record for the
  new Clerk user ID instead of erroring (covers dev DB reuse / account
  recreation scenarios)
- toMockEvent: use || instead of ?? for optional string fields so empty
  strings from the DB coerce to undefined, preventing the React Native
  "Unexpected text node cannot be a child of a View" crash when
  slotDuration, recurringDescription, or instagramHandle is ''

commit.message:
fix API error handling, self-knowledge false positives, and contact form parsing

- api/main.py: wrap pipeline in try/except with full traceback logging,
  accept both message/query fields, return both response/answer fields,
  support stateless chat_history from frontend
- api/chat.py: include answer field in error responses for frontend display
- stage4_retrieval_nodes.py: tighten self-knowledge keywords to prevent
  false-positive injection on normal project queries (e.g. "built",
  "error", "github", "crush" no longer match)
- stage1_intent_router.py: fix contact form regex to avoid crossing
  newlines, add label-based phone extraction fallback

commit.message:
feat: Notebook + Kalendář – 3 vylepšení v2.51.11

1) Kalendář/Poznámky – auto-save: NotePanel nyní automaticky ukládá
   po 1s nečinnosti (debounce) a okamžitě při opuštění editoru.
   Přidán saveTimerRef, triggerAutoSave(), localTasksRef; všechny
   akce úkolů (toggle/update/add/remove) triggerují auto-save.
   handleBlur s relatedTarget guard zabraňuje zbytečnému linkify
   při pohybu focusu uvnitř panelu.

2) Poznámky/Kódový blok – min-height: <pre> blok má nově
   min-height:2.5em takže po kliknutí (smazání placeholderu)
   výška neskolabuje. Opraveno v NoteEditor i CalEventNoteEditor.

3) Poznámky/Inline task bloky: Nové tlačítko v toolbaru (checklist
   ikona) vkládá blok úkolů kamkoliv do textu poznámky přes
   document.execCommand('insertHTML'). Více bloků v jedné poznámce.
   Enter = nový úkol, Backspace na prázdném = smazat položku.
   Kliknutí na ☐/☑ přepíná stav, × maže položku, + přidává.
   CSS: nb-tb-del fade-in on hover, nb-tb-txt placeholder.
   htmlToPlainText extrahuje inline task bloky DOM-based.
   relatedTarget guard v handleEditorBlur/handleBlur zabraňuje
   korupci DOM při přechodu mezi nb-tb-txt elementy.
   Funguje konzistentně v NoteEditor, CalEventNoteEditor i NotePanel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve digests when aborting a run

delete_run removed the date-keyed digest row, but on re-runs the
upsert overwrites run_id -- so aborting would destroy a previous
day's digest that happened to share the date. Digests are published
content and should survive run cleanup.

commit.message:
fix: replace HTML regex stripping with BeautifulSoup in prepare_for_web

The feedback div regex assumed exactly one nesting level and would
break silently if the structure changed. DOM parsing handles arbitrary
nesting correctly. Replaced all regex-based element removal with
CSS selectors via bs4 (lxml was already a transitive dep via premailer).

fix: trampolín bat usa goto+call en vez de if() para compatibilidad CMD

El if (...) con ejecución de otro .bat dentro falla en Windows CMD.
Cambiado a goto :ya_en_temp + call para flujo más robusto.
También _CONSIG_DIR usa %~dp0. (con punto) para evitar problema
con barra final y comillas.
